title: ARPA-H Commits $144 Million to PROSPR Program for Aging Clinical Trials
description: ARPA-H awarded $144M to seven teams to build infrastructure for phase 3 trials of health span interventions, including rapamycin, semaglutide, and SGLT-2 inhibitors in healthy adults in their 60s.

title: Oxford Study: Rapamycin Protects Against Age-Triggering DNA Damage in T Cells
description: University of Oxford researchers found that low-dose rapamycin (1mg/day) reduces T cell DNA damage and senescence markers in older adults by inhibiting mTOR, doubling T cell survival rates.

title: Sinclair Highlights Partial Reprogramming Conserved from Insects to Mammals
description: David Sinclair shared a new study showing partial epigenetic reprogramming works across species from insects to mammals, calling it 'a big deal' for the rejuvenation field.

title: Lifespan.io Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026
description: Monthly roundup covering Life Biosciences FDA clearance for cellular reprogramming, stem cell therapy phase 2b results for frailty, resistance exercise brain aging clocks, and rapamycin ovarian aging.

title: Rhonda Patrick: Multivitamin Supplementation Slows Biological Aging
description: Dr. Rhonda Patrick shared data showing daily multivitamin use for 2 years reduced epigenetic aging by 0.1-0.2 years vs placebo, with strongest effects in those with accelerated biological aging.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Labiotech profiles key longevity companies including Altos Labs ($3B), NewLimit, Life Biosciences, Rejuvenate Bio, and Genflow Biosciences working on reprogramming, gene therapy, and senolytics.

title: Life Biosciences Secures First FDA-Cleared Human Reprogramming Trial
description: Life Biosciences received FDA approval for a Phase 1 trial using partial epigenetic reprogramming (ER-100) to restore vision in glaucoma and NAION patients, enrolling first patients within months.

title: Peter Ottsjo: Trial Design Is the Most Important Longevity Development Right Now
description: Biotech journalist argues that advances in clinical trial architecture for aging -- not any single drug -- represent the field's most critical current development for translating animal data to humans.

title: PEARL Trial: Low-Dose Weekly Rapamycin Shows Safety and Lean Mass Benefits in Women
description: 48-week RCT showed intermittent low-dose rapamycin (10mg weekly) significantly improved lean tissue mass and pain in aging women with no increase in serious adverse events vs placebo.

title: Longevity Technology: New Documentary Features Leading Geroscientists
description: New documentary featuring leading geroscientists examines lifestyle, longevity research, and the shifting public conversation around aging, gaining traction on social media.

title: ScienceDaily: Massive Cellular Atlas Shows How Aging Reshapes the Body Across 21 Organs
description: Scientists built a comprehensive cellular atlas studying nearly 7 million cells across 21 organs, revealing how aging reshapes the body at the cellular level.

title: Longevity Investing in 2026: Strategic Guide for Family Offices
description: Comprehensive analysis of the $30.7B longevity biotech market, covering epigenetic reprogramming maturation, senolytic advances, organ regeneration progress, and AI-driven drug discovery acceleration.

title: AFAR Webinar: Unpacking Biohacking for Longevity -- March 12
description: American Federation for Aging Research hosting free webinar on biohacking for longevity on March 12, covering the intersection of consumer health optimization and scientific evidence.

title: Insilico Medicine AI Benchmark: Testing Frontier Models on Aging Biology
description: Insilico Medicine developed a new benchmark testing frontier AI models on human biology tasks relevant to aging research. Gemini ranked first overall except in two areas.

title: Stanford: Valter Longo Grand Rounds on Fasting-Mimicking Diets and Longevity
description: Stanford Department of Medicine hosting Valter Longo for a grand rounds lecture on March 11 covering fasting-mimicking diets and longevity research to transform healthy aging.

title: Sinclair Warns: HAGR Aging Genomic Resources at Risk of Going Offline
description: David Sinclair urged support for the Human Ageing Genomic Resources (HAGR) databases used by 200K+ researchers annually, which face shutdown due to lost UK government funding.

title: Daily Low-Dose Rapamycin Shows Cardiovascular Benefits in 8-Week Pilot
description: Pilot study of six men aged 70-76 taking 1mg/day rapamycin for 8 weeks showed significant improvements in diastolic heart function, peak filling rates, and blood acceleration via cardiac MRI.

title: GLP-1 Drugs Proposed as First True Longevity Medications
description: Nature Biotechnology argues GLP-1 drugs target many hallmarks of aging across multiple organs, reduce all-cause mortality, and may be the first FDA-approved class qualifying as gerotherapeutics.

title: SGLT2 Inhibitors Shown to Lengthen Telomeres in Human Study
description: A 6-month SGLT2 inhibitor intervention produced robust telomere length increases in humans, challenging the notion that telomere dynamics are unidirectional and opening doors for cellular aging reversal.

title: Fight Aging: Fenofibrate as PPAR-alpha Agonist Slows Aging in Mice via CPT1C
description: New research shows fenofibrate, a PPAR-alpha agonist typically used for lipids, slows aging in mice through the CPT1C pathway, adding another repurposed drug to the longevity toolkit.

title: Norn Group: Industrializing Longevity with $34M in Research Grants
description: Martin Borch Jensen of Norn Group discusses systematically targeting bottlenecks in longevity research with $34M in Impetus Grants, talent programs, and a clinical trial framework for aging.

title: VitaDAO: Aubrai AI Becomes State-of-the-Art for Longevity Research
description: VitaDAO's Aubrai AI, trained on decades of aging research and Dr. Aubrey de Grey's lab data, now ranks first on the BixBench benchmark for longevity biology, outperforming other AI research agents.

title: Rhonda Patrick: Post-Exercise Sauna Use Amplifies VO2 Max and Muscle Growth Signaling
description: Dr. Rhonda Patrick shared evidence that 15 minutes of sauna after aerobic exercise produces greater VO2 max improvements than training alone over 8 weeks, with emerging support for strength gains.

title: Fund Longevity: Multi-City Rally for Life Extension Planned for April 8
description: A multinational rally movement planned for April 8, 2026 aims to bring life extension advocacy into the mainstream, calling for major increases in aging research funding and regulatory pathways.

title: Nature: Human Hippocampal Neurogenesis and Cognitive Resilience in SuperAgers
description: Published in Nature, researchers found that 'SuperAgers' who maintain exceptional cognition past 80 show preserved hippocampal neurogenesis, suggesting brain cell renewal is key to cognitive resilience.
